context.street_character: One-way EB | 40 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks | Mixed commercial-residential

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: red-brown brick with cream trim, red-painted bargeboards and portico details, plus mottled grey stone veneer at the lower facade
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Historic form remains legible, but the facade has seen piecemeal alterations including stone veneer and replacement elements.
photo_observations.window_details: Large front window at ground level, paired upper windows, and a small decorative octagonal window set beside the entry portico.
photo_observations.door_details: White front door beneath an ornate red-painted wood portico with curved brackets and trim.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative vergeboards and painted gable trim are the main visible cornice elements.
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Shallow projecting bay on the ground floor beneath the front gable.
photo_observations.porch_details: Raised front stoop with a compact covered portico and simple white railings.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney visible in the cropped view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Octagonal window, cut-out gable ornament, and curved painted trim give the house a highly decorative Victorian character.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Stone veneer at grade, replacement door and windows, and simplified railings alter the original frontage.
photo_observations.notes: Observed from a frontal residential streetscape view.

notes: Observed from a frontal residential streetscape view.

facade_detail.composition: The street frontage spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys beneath a cross-gable, consistent with its commercial, converted house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Key storefront and facade features include decorative brick, original windows, bay window, cornice.

facade_description: Two-storey converted semi-detached house, pre-1889, with mixed facade materials: red-brown brick upper and mottled grey stone veneer at grade. Ornate red-painted wood portico with curved brackets, octagonal accent window, and decorative gable trim. Cross-gable roof, party wall on left side only; 3 dwelling units. Fair condition with piecemeal alterations visible.
